LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF DENNIS MANGANO'S MOTION TO SET HEARING RE PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED AS UNNECESSARY. PETITIONER DOES NOT NEED A COURT ORDER TO CALENDAR HIS PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. PETITIONER MAY FILE AND SERVE ITS MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AS A REGULAR LAW AND MOTION MATTER. THE COURT HEARS SUCH MATTERS MONDAY THROUGH FRIDAY AT 9:30 A.M. ALL PARTIES ARE TO LODGE COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR FILINGS IN COMPLIANCE WITH SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6B. THE FILINGS SHOULD COMPLY WITH CCP 1005(B). JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

MANGANO IS INSTRUCTED TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF HIS OPPOSITION TO DEPARTMENT 220, WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THIS TENTATIVE RULING, NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 1, 2013 AT 4:00 PM. LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING WRIT OF MANDATE IS GRANTED. ON AUGUST 26, 2015, DEFENDANT UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA SAN FRANCISCO FILED A NOTICE OF NON-OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFF DENNIS MANGANO'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING WRIT OF MANDATE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =302/EG

LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGE KAHN MADE A DISCLOSURE ABOUT BEING A GOOD FRIED WITH THE GENERAL COUNSEL FOR THE REGENTS, BUT HE BELIEVES THAT IT DOES NOT HAVE ANY EFFECT ON HIS ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. DEFENDANTS MERVYN MAZE AND THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE FIRST THROUGH THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO MERVYN MAZE AND WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO THE REGENTS. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION. AS TO MR. MAZE, NONE OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION LIE SINCE IT IS THE REGENTS, IF ANYONE, WHO HAS OR CAN TAKE ACTION WITH REGARD TO PLAINTIFF'S POSITION. PLAINTIFF IS ONLY SEEKING DAMAGES UNDER THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, WHICH IS NOT AVAILABLE. (KATZBERG V. UC REGENTS (2002) 29 CAL.4TH 300; LU V. HAWAIIAN GARDENS CASINO, INC. (2010) 50 CAL.4TH 592, 602). AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF'S REMEDY IS TO PURSUE A MANDAMUS CLAIM. (CCP 1061). AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF CANNOT PURSUE THE COMMON LAW WRONGFUL TERMINATION CAUSE OF ACTION BUT HAS LEAVE TO PLEAD A CLAIM WITH A STATUTORY BASIS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Sep-25-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF DENNIS MANGANO'S Motion For Order Granting Writ Of Mandate is Granted. On August 26, 2015, Defendant University of California San Francisco filed a Notice of Non-Opposition to Plaintiff Dennis Mangano's Motion for Order Granting Writ of Mandate. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute
Off Calendar May-07-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on the matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute is Denied without prejudice to being refiled on or after November 9, 2015 if a hearing on a writ has not been scheduled by that date. There is a strong policy to hear cases on the merits, settlement discussions between the parties have led to some delay in prosecution of the case, and plaintiff recently retained counsel who represents that he intends to calendar a hearing on a writ of mandate in the near future. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185

Plaintiff Dennis Mangano'S Motion To Set Hearing Re Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Nov-22-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Dennis Mangano's Motion To Set Hearing Re Petition For Writ Of Mandate is denied as unnecessary. Petitioner does not need a court order to calendar his petition for writ of mandate. Petitioner may file and serve its motion for writ of mandate as a regular law and motion matter. The Court hears such matters Monday through Friday at 9:30 a.m. All parties are to lodge courtesy copies of their filings in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The filings should comply with CCP 1005(b). Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Judge Miller is recused. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-02-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BENJAMIN J. MEISELAS OF THE LAW OFFICES OF GERAGOS & GERAGOS, TEL. NO. (213) 625-3900 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHAEL A. LAURENSON OF GORDON & REES LLP, TEL. NO. 986-5900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT UC REGENTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. Based on the first amended complaint, the Court cannot determine whether the section 820.2 immunity applies because it is not clear what defendant's alleged basic policy decision was. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-16-2012 Continued To May-18-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-20-2012 Continued To Mar-29-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Mervyn Maze, The Regents Of The University Of California'S Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-09-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael A. Laurenson, Esq., from Gordon & Rees LLP, 415-986-5900, for Defendant and moving party
Benjamin J. Meiselas, Esq., from Geragos & Geragos, 213-625-3900, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Judge Kahn made a disclosure about being a good fried with the General Counsel for the Regents, but he believes that it does not have any effect on his ability to be fair and impartial. No objections were made.
Argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling as modified.
Defendants Mervyn Maze and The Regents Of The University Of California'S Demurrer To Complaint is Sustained as to the first trhough third causes of action without leave to amend as to Mervyn Maze and with ten days leave to amend as to the third cause of action as to the Regents.
The demurrer is overruled as to the fourth cause of action.
As to Mr. Maze, none of the causes of action lie since it is the Regents, if anyone, who has or can take action with regard to plaintiff's position. Plaintiff is only seeking damages under the first cause of action, which is not available. (Katzberg v. UC Regents (2002) 29 Cal.4th 300; Lu v. Hawaiian Gardens Casino, Inc. (2010) 50 Cal.4th 592, 602). As to the second cause of action, plaintiff's remedy is to pursue a mandamus claim. (CCP 1061). As to the third cause of action, plaintiff cannot pursue the common law wrongful termination cause of action but has leave to plead a claim with a statutory basis. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077
